documentation.xhtml: thermal/cook acceleration in sim (1.0 = real time)
tray pick grid (chamber tray held by fork)
TRUE = param writes go to the real drive
TODO calibrate: drive gear -> pulses per mm gap
PR path table base TODO verify in CS2RS manual
regs per path block TODO verify
path pos HIGH word offset TODO verify
path pos LOW word offset TODO verify
---- Subsystem IDs for ErrorID = SHL(ss,16) OR code ----

============================================================ GVL_Cfg — machine constants. VAR_GLOBAL CONSTANT. SIM_MODE is the ONLY switch between simulation and hardware. Set FALSE for production download. Never change at runtime. ============================================================
per-press smash/hold position trim, mm (+ = larger gap); HMI-adjustable, clamped to +/- GVL_Cfg.PRESS_TRIM_MAX_MM in PRG_HMI

============================================================ GVL_Par — retained tunables (recipes, offsets). HMI-writable through bounds-checked paths only (PRG_HMI / PRG_GatewayComms). ============================================================

============================================================ GVL_IO — the ONLY POU with physical addresses. SIM NOTE: in SIM_MODE these are never read/written (PRG_IoMap short-circuits). Addresses below are PLACEHOLDERS — replace with the real electrical map during panel design (see MIGRATION_GUIDE.md step 2) and keep wire numbers in comments. ============================================================

============================================================ GVL_Axes — SoftMotion axis references. SIMULATION BUILD: the dummy declarations below let the project compile with SM3_Basic present but no drives; FB_AxisCtrl never calls MC_* while SIM_MODE=TRUE. PRODUCTION BUILD: DELETE these dummies and pass the device-tree axis objects (names created by the EtherCAT config) directly in PRG_Motion — see MIGRATION_GUIDE.md step 3. ============================================================
+/- mm added to that press's trim
LT2100E Modbus-TCP register image (see HMI_LT2100E_GUIDE.md) HMI writes 600..609, reads 620..669. Packed/unpacked in PRG_HMI.
default TRUE in sim; set FALSE for gateway runs
rising edge starts the automated suite

============================================================ GVL_Cmd — commands INTO the PLC (Gateway + HMI + test). Modbus-TCP register images live here (%MW). The MC508 Modbus TCP slave exposes %MW to the Gateway. ============================================================

============================================================ GVL_Mech — ALL FB instances live here (single ownership table). BODY-CALL OWNERSHIP (call the FB body ONLY in the listed PRG): PRG_Motion (T_Motion): axis FBs, fbGantry, fbScraper, fbFork, fbGripper PRG_Carousels(T_App) : press drives, presses, VFDs, carousels, pneumatics, airlock PRG_Heating (T_App) : heaters, heat bank, refrigeration All other POUs only WRITE inputs / READ outputs of these instances. ============================================================
light-contact gap for remainder of cook
===== FUNCTION F_Err : UDINT ===== ErrorID encoding: (subsystem << 16) OR code

ST.xhtml: F_Err := SHL(TO_UDINT(ss), 16) OR TO_UDINT(code);

documentation.xhtml: ===== FUNCTION F_RaiseAlarm : BOOL ===== Latch an alarm into GVL_Alm (dedup by id). Returns TRUE if new.

============================================================ FB_AxisCtrl — single-axis wrapper. The ONLY POU calling MC_*. Runs in T_Motion. One instance per axis, body called EVERY cycle. SIM: internal kinematic model (trapezoid-less: const-vel ramp). REAL: SoftMotion CiA-402 via SM3_Basic. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_PidHeater — PI + time-proportioned SSR + TC plausibility. Call every T_App cycle; internal divider executes PI at 100 ms. SIM: first-order plant dT = (gain*out - (T-Tamb)/tau) * dt Independent hardwired thermostat is the REAL protection; the over-temp check here is early warning (INFO/ZONE only). ============================================================

============================================================ FB_PneuActuator — valve + 2 reed/prox sensors + timeout. Cmd TRUE = extend/open, FALSE = retract/close. Runs in T_App. SIM: sensors flip after SIM_TRAVEL_S. ============================================================
edge: push the 3 positions to the drive
drive path table matches last written values
at least one successful full push done

============================================================ FB_PressDrive — CS2RS in PR mode, DI-trigger control. Validated pattern from the gantry test project: - set path-select DOs, wait one cycle, raise CTRG and HOLD it until InPos confirms (never a single-scan pulse) - InPos handshake = "saw moving" (InPos drops, then returns) Modbus RTU monitoring (position/alarm) is DIAGNOSTIC ONLY and added at migration (stub hooks below). Runs in T_App (20 ms). PR paths (configure in drive, MIGRATION_GUIDE step 4): path 0 = OPEN position, path 1 = SMASH pos, path 2 = HOLD pos ============================================================

============================================================ FB_VfdIndex — carousel indexing: VFD run/dir + encoder + pin. Pin actuator is external (FB_PneuActuator); this FB commands it via PinCmd and reads PinExtended/PinRetracted. Sequence: retract pin -> rotate (fast->creep) -> stop in window -> extend pin -> verify pin + encoder plausibility. Runs in T_App. SIM: integrates encoder; home prox modeled. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_Airlock — chamber gate mutual exclusion. Requests are LEVELS; the FB grants at most one open door, closing the other first. ChamberSealed = both confirmed closed. Runs in T_App. DoorFridge = fridge-side gate, DoorChamber = griddle-side gate. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_Fork — tray fetch/return, 2 axes + fail-safe brake. Commands: MCMD_HOME, MCMD_FETCH(p1=level), MCMD_RETURN(p1=level). Fetch: Z to level -> tele out -> micro-lift (release stacking lock) -> tele in (tray drawn into chamber). Return: Z to level+micro -> tele out -> micro-drop (re-engage lock) -> tele in. Brake: qBrakeRelease TRUE only while Z powered & healthy; a feedback mismatch is MACHINE_STOP class. Runs in T_Motion. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_FridgeCarousel — tray column indexing (2 faces). Identical pattern to the griddle carousel, no rotation permissive beyond fork-retracted (wired by caller as Permissive). Unidirectional is acceptable here. Runs in T_App. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_Gantry — pick/place mechanism. Standard interface (spec sec.12). Owns sequencing only; axis FB bodies are called in PRG_Motion. Commands: MCMD_HOME, MCMD_PICK(p1=trayIdx 0..23), MCMD_PLACE(p1=slot 0..5), MCMD_PARK, MCMD_SANITIZE. Coordinated XY = simultaneous X + theta targets (v1; swap to interpolated transform after IK validation without touching callers — see method note in spec 9.1). Runs in T_Motion. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_GriddleCarousel — thin coordinator over FB_VfdIndex + pin FB_PneuActuator with the ROTATION PERMISSIVE interlock: all presses open + gantry parked + scraper parked (computed by PRG_JobExec, wired in PRG_Carousels). ActStation = index of the griddle currently AT THE LOAD STATION. Griddle g is at scrape station when ActStation = (g+2) MOD 4. Runs in T_App. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_Gripper — v1 stub over one pneumatic channel. INTERFACE IS FROZEN: swap internals when the real effector is designed. Owned pneu FB instance is external (GVL_Mech.fbPneuGrip), body called in PRG_Carousels; this FB only writes Cmd / reads status. Runs in T_Motion (with FB_Gantry). ============================================================

============================================================ FB_HeatBank — supervises 8 FB_PidHeater zones: stagger tokens (inrush cap during ramp) + aggregate status. Call AFTER the heater bodies each T_App cycle (one-cycle token lag is fine). Zone map: griddle g -> zones 2g (top), 2g+1 (bottom). ============================================================

ST.xhtml: nRamping := 0; AllAtSet := Enable; MaxDevC := 0.0; AnyTcErr := FALSE; FOR z := 0 TO 7 DO ramping := (Heaters[z].Zone.setC - Heaters[z].Zone.actC) > 20.0; IF ramping THEN IF nRamping < GVL_Cfg.HEAT_MAX_STAGGER THEN Token[z] := TRUE; nRamping := nRamping + 1; ELSE Token[z] := FALSE; END_IF ELSE Token[z] := TRUE; // regulation always allowed END_IF dev := ABS(Heaters[z].Zone.setC - Heaters[z].Zone.actC); IF dev > MaxDevC THEN MaxDevC := dev; END_IF IF NOT Heaters[z].Zone.atSet THEN AllAtSet := FALSE; END_IF IF Heaters[z].Error THEN AnyTcErr := TRUE; END_IF END_FOR IF NOT Enable THEN AllAtSet := FALSE; END_IF

documentation.xhtml: ============================================================ FB_IKTransform — Cartesian (x,y) -> gantry (xLinear, thetaDeg). Model: carriage travels along X at y=0; arm of length ARM_LEN pivots on carriage: x = xLin + L*cos(theta), y = L*sin(theta) Solution: theta = ASIN(y/L) (elbow toward +x), xLin = x - L*cos. MILESTONE M1 GATE: validate over the full tray+griddle table in simulation (see TESTING_GUIDE TC-02) before any hardware motion. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_Press — one clamshell press. Wraps FB_PressDrive (paths: 0=OPEN 1=SMASH 2=HOLD). Executes the force/position profile: CLOSE->SMASH (smashDurS) -> BACK OFF to HOLD (light contact) -> stays HOLDING until MCMD_OPEN. Product-quality decision: never full force for the whole cook. Runs in T_App. Commands: MCMD_HOME(=open), MCMD_PROFILE, MCMD_OPEN. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_Refrigeration — compressor hysteresis + HACCP excursion timer. Runs in T_App. SIM: simple pulldown/heat-leak model. Excursion (> setpoint+0.5 C for FRIDGE_EXC_S) -> QUARANTINE flag (mapped by PRG_Alarms). ============================================================

============================================================ FB_Scraper — sweep mechanism. Commands: MCMD_HOME, MCMD_PARK, MCMD_SWEEP(p1=strokeIdx 0..2). One SWEEP = position at row, Z engage, X sweep across, Z up, return. Caller (JobExec) guarantees the interlock: griddle at scrape station, clamshell open, press retracted. Runs in T_Motion. ============================================================
consumer (PRG_HACCP) sets after reading the record

============================================================ FB_GriddleTracker — per-griddle state + cook timers + HACCP record emission. Runs in T_App (called from PRG_JobExec's PRG). ScrapeDue fires at cookTime - SCRAPE_GUARD_S so the coordinator can stage the scraper. NewHaccp is a LATCHED flag: set here, cleared by the consumer (PRG_HACCP, slower task) after reading. ============================================================

============================================================ FB_HomingManager — collision-safe machine homing (spec 8.3): presses OPEN -> gantry -> scraper -> fork -> griddle carousel -> fridge carousel. Runs in T_App (called by PRG_Supervisor, which executes BEFORE PRG_Carousels in the task list). ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_JobExec — executes Gateway primitives (T_App, after Supervisor). Enforces ORDER SAFETY only; scheduling policy is the Gateway's. Autonomous behavior: if a cook completes and the Gateway is silent (or no command pending), scrape locally — patties never burn waiting for a dead PC. In testMode this program idles (PRG_Test has authority). Primitive sub-sequences use numbered CASE states: 100+ FETCH_TRAY 200+ LOAD_GRIDDLE 300+ SCRAPE 400+ RETURN_TRAY ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_Supervisor — machine mode state machine (T_App, runs FIRST among coordinators). Owns: mode transitions, enable distribution, abort broadcast, homing orchestration, clean-mode thermal gate. Safety is only MONITORED here (GVL_Sts.safetyOk). ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_Alarms — central alarm manager (T_App, runs LAST). Raises on the RISING edge of each source Error (so an acked alarm doesn't re-raise from a still-latched source; sources clear their Error on the next command latch / axis reset). Class map (spec sec.13): MACHINE_STOP : fork brake/drive integrity ZONE_STOP : axis/drive faults, pin mismatch, TC fault, airlock violation, mechanism timeouts QUARANTINE : fridge excursion, short-cook (via PRG_HACCP) INFO : gateway silent ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_GatewayComms — Modbus-TCP register map + seq/ack handshake (T_Comms, 100 ms). PLC is Modbus TCP slave; Gateway reads/writes the %MW images declared in GVL_Cmd. Register map (holding registers, 1 reg = 1 WORD): CMD %MW0..9 : [0]=seq [1]=code [2..3]=p1 lo/hi [4..5]=p2 [6..7]=p3 [8]=p4(lo only) [9]=gwHeartbeat ACK %MW10..15 : [10]=seqEcho [11]=flags(b0 busy b1 done b2 err) [12..13]=errorId lo/hi [14]=plc heartbeat STS %MW20..99 : see packing below PAR %MW500.. : [500]=item [501]=cookTimeS*10 [502]=setC*10 [503]=applyToggle (change -> apply, READY only) ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_HACCP — cook record collector (T_Comms). RETAIN ring of 512 records (> one shift). Gateway currently reads the LATEST record snapshot from STS regs; full drain protocol via read pointer is a marked TODO for Gateway integration (M4). ============================================================
TODO site-specific, move to secure store
jog levels (direct var OR HMI register bit)
jog levels (direct var OR HMI register bit)

============================================================ PRG_HMI — HMI request edges + password-gated jog (T_Comms). Buttons arrive as GVL_Cmd.hmi* (mapped to HMI tags at site). NOTE: in the SFC/LD refactor this POU's edge networks become LD rungs one-per-button (technician-facing). ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_Carousels — T_App body-call owner: press drives, presses, pneumatics, VFD indexers, carousels, airlock. Pure wiring (the ST equivalent of a CFC sheet). Runs AFTER Supervisor/ JobExec/Test (which write the Cmd inputs). ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_Heating — T_App body-call owner: 8 heaters + bank + fridge. Zone map: griddle g -> zones 2g (top), 2g+1 (bottom). Setpoint follows the active recipe of the griddle's item; empty griddles idle at the BEEF setpoint so they are ready to load. ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_IoMap — T_App, runs FIRST. Housekeeping + the place for commissioning force-rungs (these networks become LD at the language refactor). All %I/%Q symbol reads/writes elsewhere go through the owner wiring PRGs; addresses exist ONLY in GVL_IO. ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_Motion — T_Motion (4 ms). Body-call owner for all axis FBs and the motion mechanisms. THE ONLY TASK TOUCHING MC_* / AXIS_REF. This is the ST equivalent of the CFC wiring diagram: pure parameter assignment + instance calls, no logic. MIGRATION: replace GVL_Axes.axRef* with the device-tree axis objects created by the EtherCAT scan (step 3 of the guide). ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_Sim — T_App. Plant models beyond what L1 drivers model internally. v1: reserved (axis/pneu/thermal/fridge models are inside their driver FBs; patty bookkeeping is in JobExec). Extend here for: patty-present optics model, condensation / door-open thermal coupling, disturbance injection. ============================================================

============================================================ PRG_Test — automated regression suite (T_App, after JobExec). Start: set GVL_Cmd.testStart := TRUE (rising edge) once mode has reached READY. Results: aResult[n] : 0=pending 1=PASS 2=FAIL (n = 1..10) aFailCode[n] : diagnostic (step*1000 or ErrorID low word) PassCount / suiteDone TC list: 1 boot->READY + allHomed 6 griddle carousel 1,3,0 2 IK table (24 pick + 6 place) 7 fork fetch/return L5 3 heat AtSet + fridge + stagger 8 gantry pick/place/park 4 airlock mutual exclusion 9 full cycle via Gateway regs 5 press smash->hold->open 10 fault inject + recovery x2 ============================================================

Library.Name: #BreakpointLogging
Library.DefaultResolution: Breakpoint Logging Functions, 3.5.5.0 (3S - Smart Software Solutions GmbH)

Value.name: _x0034_00010
Value.desc: Devices of the LocalBus master device

Parameter.Name: LocalBusMaster Info
Parameter.Description: Devices of the LocalBus master device

Value.name: _x0038_05306369
Value.desc: Show the logger page for the master

Parameter.Name: ShowLoggerPage
Parameter.Description: Show the logger page for the master

Value.name: _x0038_05392650
Value.desc: true: transmit only ARP broadcasts, false: transmit all broadcasts

Parameter.Name: OnlyArpBroadcasts
Parameter.Description: true: transmit only ARP broadcasts, false: transmit all broadcasts

Value.name: _x0038_05458944
Value.desc: Send frame at task cycle start

Parameter.Name: FrameAtTaskStart
Parameter.Description: Send frame at task cycle start

Value.name: _x0038_05458960
Value.desc: Wait for packets in the same cycle/ FrameAtTaskStart must be also set

Parameter.Name: WaitForPacket
Parameter.Description: Wait for packets in the same cycle/ FrameAtTaskStart must be also set

Value.name: _x0038_05458968
Value.desc: Splits cylic frames in process data and asynchronous messages

Parameter.Name: SplitFrame
Parameter.Description: Splits cylic frames in process data and asynchronous messages

Value.name: _x0038_05459968
Value.desc: Export ENI file with custom settings

Parameter.Description: Export ENI file with custom settings

Value.name: _x0038_05459969
Value.desc: Ignore manual address settings in the ENI export

Parameter.Name: IgnoreManualAddress
Parameter.Description: Ignore manual address settings in the ENI export

Value.desc: Number of Slaves with an Output

Parameter.Description: Number of Slaves with an Output

Value.desc: Number of Slaves with an Input
